audience: people who blame retrograde for a week of crossed wires in their group chat
insight: at peak retrograde the group chat is where every misfire lands, and rendering that week back as one deadpan incident report travels further than complaining about it
assetXElement: your group chat x an auto-generated retrograde incident report of this week's crossed wires
successMetric: peak-week share rate of the incident report card

referenceSet.entry: Hook pattern 1, the number claim: lead with a specific odd number of incidents instead of a round one so it reads as measured rather than invented

generationPrompts.image: Editorial tech cover with a defocused photographic background: a saturated deep-blue color core melting into shadow toward the edges, visible depth of field, subtle film grain, no flat fills. Centered in generous negative space, a single clean report card floats in space titled RETROGRADE INCIDENT REPORT, with three short redacted-looking log lines and a small timestamp. Add crisp white functional line strokes for one small constellation in the corner only, minimal and purposeful, no decorative flourishes. No logos, no watermarks. 16:9.
generationPrompts.motion: [for ChatCut] three shot script: shot 1 establishes a group chat scrolling fast with overlapping notifications in everyday chaos; shot 2 introduces the new element as contrast, the notifications collapse and reorganize into tidy numbered log lines; shot 3 lands the hook, the report card tilts to camera and the hook line locks in as on-screen copy, 6 seconds total.
generationPrompts.copy: Write one hook line, 18 words or fewer, deadpan, framing the week's group chat chaos as a calmly filed report, no exclamation marks, no em dash.
